# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 11 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.7 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 2 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 12 rounds.
Players can identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 4.8 and split equally among all active players.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 20 rounds.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 4 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 12 rounds.
Players can identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 7.5 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 1 coin to the source and deducts 3 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 4.8 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 6 coins from the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 20 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.

Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 7.5 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 3.3 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 5 coins from the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.3 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 10 rounds.
Players can identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

Players do not see others' choices before deciding.

Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 25 rounds.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 11 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 25 rounds.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 4.4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 4 coins to the source and deducts 7 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 4 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 6 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 22 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 4 coins to the source and deducts 11 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 6 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 2 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.8 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 11 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 4 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 17 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 6.4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 16 rounds.
Players cannot identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.2 and split equally among all active players.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 5 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 2 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.2 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 7 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 3.4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 4 coins to the source and deducts 7 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 6.4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 1 coin to the source and deducts 2 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 16 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.

Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.

However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 2.4 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 10 coins from the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.7 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 3 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 2 coins to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 7 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 7.8 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 4 coins to the source and deducts 11 coins from the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 26 rounds.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

### ROUND 29 SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.2 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 9 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.2 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 11.3 and split equally among all active players.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 4.7 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 2 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 29 rounds.
Players can identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 11.3 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 4 coins from the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 4.7 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 8 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 2 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 29 rounds.
Players can identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and chooses an integer contribution from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 7.5 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 5 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 1 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 8.9 and split equally among all active players.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 15 rounds.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 8.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 4 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 15 rounds.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 9.6 and split equally among all active players.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 5 rounds.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 11.7 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 4 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 9.6 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 3 coins from the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 5 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 11.7 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 4 coins to the source and deducts 9 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 4 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.5 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 1 coin to the source and deducts 1 coin from the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 23 rounds.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 12.1 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 3 coins to the source and deducts 7 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 27 rounds.
Players can identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 7.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 4 coins from the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players can identify who punished them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing other players and amount deducted from punishment.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each player receives 20 coins in private holdings each round and must contribute either 0 or 20 to the shared pot.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 6.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 1 coin to the source and deducts 2 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 12.1 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 3 coins to the source and adds 2 coins to the target.
Players cannot send group chat messages in this game.
Players know that the game lasts 27 rounds.
Players can identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 7.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 2 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

### ROUND 21 SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, so the resulting contribution must be either 0 or 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 7.9 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 2 coins to the source and deducts 3 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 2 coins to the source and adds 3 coins to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players do not know the total number of rounds while playing.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 1.1 and split equally among all active players.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 12 rounds.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round.
However, the corresponding information of the peers are not shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 3.1 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may reward other players.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 8 rounds.
Players cannot identify who rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for rewarding other players and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.

# GAME RULES
This is an online public goods game (PGG).
Each round, the 20 coins begin in the shared pot. Each player chooses how many coins to withdraw for private use, and the remainder becomes their contribution. Contributions are integers from 0 to 20.
Players do not see others' choices before deciding.
The shared pot is multiplied by 3.1 and split equally among all active players.
After contributions are revealed and redistributed, players may punish and reward other players.
Each punishment unit costs 1 coin to the source and deducts 2 coins from the target.
Each reward unit costs 1 coin to the source and adds 1 coin to the target.
Players may send messages to the group whenever they choose to do so.
Players know that the game lasts 8 rounds.
Players cannot identify who punished/rewarded them in their summary information.
At the summary stage (`ROUND N SUMMARY SHOWN TO PLAYERS`), each player sees their own net payoff of the round, along with the amount they used for punishing and rewarding other players, amount deducted from punishment, and amount received for reward.
The corresponding information of the peers are also shown.
